---
title: "Ungraded Practice"
---

This page gives optional practice prompts connected to the book, slides, and notebooks.

::: {.callout-important}
These activities are for practice and preparation. They are **not separate graded assignments** unless the instructor explicitly announces otherwise. Course grading is governed by the [syllabus](syllabus.qmd).
:::

The purpose is to help students keep a running record of the central habit of the course:

> What does the data support, what does the model assume, and where can the reconstruction fail?

Students may keep these notes privately, use them while studying, or bring questions from them to class.

## How To Use This Page

For each week/module:

- read the connected book chapter;
- attend both sessions and use the weekly slide deck as the discussion structure;
- run the notebook in Python or Google Colab;
- write a short four-thread or claim-evidence-limit note for yourself.

The notes are useful preparation for quizzes, exams, project work, and the oral defense, but they are not an additional grading category.

For the full class workflow connecting book, slides, notebooks, and these practice notes, see [How To Prepare For Class](preparation.qmd).

## Claim-Evidence-Limit Template

Use this short format when a notebook or class discussion produces a reconstruction result.

```text
Claim:

Evidence:

Model assumption:

Limit or failure case:
```

Example:

| Item | Example |
|---|---|
| Claim | TV denoising keeps the main edge sharper than quadratic smoothing. |
| Evidence | Gradient maps and edge contrast stay high across three TV weights. |
| Model assumption | The image is approximately piecewise smooth. |
| Limit or failure case | TV creates staircasing on smooth intensity ramps. |

## Four-Thread Notebook Note

Each notebook ends with a short four-thread note. It is the same habit in a slightly more structured form:

| Thread | Question |
|---|---|
| Measurement | What did the data record? |
| Prior | What assumption, regularizer, denoiser, or learned information entered? |
| Computation | What algorithm, update, transform, or learned map produced the result? |
| Evidence | What output, residual, metric, sweep, or failure case supports the claim? |

## Practice Guide

| Week | Topic | Ungraded Practice | Notebook Focus | Useful Links |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Images as mathematical objects | Fill the four-thread note for a masked image: observation, missing information, vectorization, and one unsupported claim. | From Scene to Data | [book](book/week-01-image-formation.qmd), [slides](slides/week-01-image-formation.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week01_image_formation.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week01_image_formation.ipynb) |
| 2 | Convolution and blur | Fill the four-thread note for blur: observed data, convolution assumption, inverse filtering, and instability evidence. | Blur as a Forward Model | [book](book/week-02-convolution-blur.qmd), [slides](slides/week-02-convolution-blur.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week02_convolution_blur.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week02_convolution_blur.ipynb) |
| 3 | Fourier transform in imaging | Fill the four-thread note for Fourier deblurring: measured spectrum, regularized frequencies, FFT computation, and artifacts. | Fourier Projection and Stability | [book](book/week-03-fourier-imaging.qmd), [slides](slides/week-03-fourier-imaging.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week03_fourier_imaging.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week03_fourier_imaging.ipynb) |
| 4 | Noise models and likelihoods | Fill the four-thread note for likelihood: noise model, missing prior, simulation or averaging, and one limit of repeated measurements. | Likelihood as Data Evidence | [book](book/week-04-noise-likelihood.qmd), [slides](slides/week-04-noise-likelihood.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week04_noise_likelihood.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week04_noise_likelihood.ipynb) |
| 5 | Ill-posed inverse problems | Fill the four-thread note for SVD directions: measurement, stability rule, truncated inverse, and trustworthy versus unstable directions. | Directions and Evidence | [book](book/week-05-ill-posed-inverse-problems.qmd), [slides](slides/week-05-ill-posed-inverse-problems.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week05_ill_posed_inverse_problems.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week05_ill_posed_inverse_problems.ipynb) |
| 6 | Tikhonov regularization | Fill the four-thread note for Tikhonov: observation, lambda prior, filtering computation, and observable diagnostics without ground truth. | Choosing Lambda Without the Truth | [book](book/week-06-tikhonov-regularization.qmd), [slides](slides/week-06-tikhonov-regularization.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week06_tikhonov_regularization.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week06_tikhonov_regularization.ipynb) |
| 7 | Variational formulation | Fill the four-thread note for one energy: data term, regularizer, descent computation, and what energy decrease proves. | Energy as an Audit Trail | [book](book/week-07-variational-formulation.qmd), [slides](slides/week-07-variational-formulation.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week07_variational_formulation.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week07_variational_formulation.ipynb) |
| 8 | Total Variation regularization | Fill the four-thread note for TV: noisy data, sparse-gradient prior, denoising sweep, and one staircasing or edge-preservation claim. | Edge Preservation Is Not Proof | [book](book/week-08-total-variation.qmd), [slides](slides/week-08-total-variation.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week08_total_variation.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week08_total_variation.ipynb) |
| 9 | Optimization methods | Fill the four-thread note for ISTA: data step, sparse prior, update rule, and convergence evidence beyond the final image. | Optimization Diagnostics | [book](book/week-09-optimization-methods.qmd), [slides](slides/week-09-optimization-methods.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week09_optimization_methods.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week09_optimization_methods.ipynb) |
| 10 | Sparse reconstruction | Fill the four-thread note for sparse DCT reconstruction and design one representation-mismatch example. | Representation Mismatch | [book](book/week-10-sparse-reconstruction.qmd), [slides](slides/week-10-sparse-reconstruction.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week10_sparse_reconstruction.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week10_sparse_reconstruction.ipynb) |
| 11 | Wavelets and multiscale representation | Fill the four-thread note for wavelet denoising: task, wavelet prior, thresholding step, and one lost detail. | Fourier, Wavelets, and Energy | [book](book/week-11-wavelets.qmd), [slides](slides/week-11-wavelets.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week11_wavelets.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week11_wavelets.ipynb) |
| 12 | Model-based vs data-driven imaging | Fill the four-thread note for learned PCA denoising and test one distribution shift. | Training Data as a Prior | [book](book/week-12-model-data.qmd), [slides](slides/week-12-model-data.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week12_model_data.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week12_model_data.ipynb) |
| 13 | Plug-and-play and learned regularization | Fill the four-thread note for PnP, including residual, fixed-point change, denoiser prior, and failure case. | Implicit Prior Strength | [book](book/week-13-plug-and-play.qmd), [slides](slides/week-13-plug-and-play.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week13_plug_and_play.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week13_plug_and_play.ipynb) |
| 14 | Stability, robustness, and ethics | Turn one four-thread note into three project-style claims with evidence and limitations. | From Notebook to Project Defense | [book](book/week-14-stability-robustness-ethics.qmd), [slides](slides/week-14-stability-robustness-ethics.qmd), [notebook](notebooks/week14_stability_robustness.ipynb), [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week14_stability_robustness.ipynb) |
| 15 | Project presentations and oral defenses | Review the model, code, evidence, limitations, and LLM-use statement for the final project. | Not applicable | [project page](project/index.qmd), [syllabus](syllabus.qmd) |

## Project Preparation Guide

The syllabus defines the assessed project moments as the proposal in Week 6, the report in Week 14, and the oral defense in Week 15. The project notes below are study and planning aids for the final modeling project described on the [project page](project/index.qmd); they do not create additional graded items.

| Week | Planning Aid |
|---:|---|
| 6 | Prepare the project proposal: topic, team, data source, baseline, second method, degradation model, and first definition of $x$, $y$, and the forward process. |
| 7 | Refine the model statement, data access, and implementation choices after feedback. |
| 8 | Make sure the notebook can generate or load data and run a simple baseline. |
| 10 | Check that both methods run on the same test cases. |
| 12 | Add one parameter sweep, one metric, and one robustness or failure test. |
| 14 | Prepare the final notebook, written report, claim-evidence table, LLM-use statement, and oral-defense explanations. |

## Grading Boundary

This page does not modify the syllabus.

Formal grading is based on the grading categories and policies stated in the [syllabus](syllabus.qmd), together with any explicit instructor announcements. Practice notes are meant to support learning and project preparation; they are not an additional hidden assessment.
